args.text: decide is a rare, high-value move that closes an issue; friction is appropriate, same gating logic used for cyclic-preference rejection (Q2)
args.author: Archivist

args.text: An extra move is friction: correcting a hasty decide now needs two steps, worse for agents
args.author: Agent

args.text: Retaining the fact in 'log' is not the point: a bare re-decide records no basis for WHY the prior was overturned. An explicit supersede --basis forces capturing that reasoning, not just the bare flip
args.author: Archivist

args.text: pudl is append-only: InvalidateFact closes the tt interval, it does not delete. 'log' still shows both decisions and the invalidation, so no history is lost
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Silent auto-invalidate hides that a reversal happened: the current view flips with no recorded rationale. Provenance outranks convenience (matches the no-edit/immutability ethos, design §5.1)
args.author: Archivist

args.text: Reject a bare re-decide; require an explicit supersede move that links back to the prior decision
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Auto-invalidate the prior decision on a new decide (last decide wins)
args.author: Proposer

args.text: When a new decide lands on an already-decided issue, auto-invalidate the prior decision or require an explicit supersede move?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: provenance: overturning a decision must record its own rationale; bare auto-flip loses the why. Matches §5.1 immutability ethos
